description: This lecture discusses issues with proof of stake (PoS) longest-chain protocols, focusing on the complications arising from variable numbers of winners in VRF-based random sampling. It highlights the impact on latency and throughput due to time slots without leaders and the challenges posed by Byzantine leaders proposing multiple blocks.
topic_summary: Exploration of PoS longest-chain consensus protocols, addressing VRF-based sampling issues, leader variability, and Byzantine leader implications on consensus properties [t=0:56 - t=337:85].
